body_excerpt: {"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-35367", "cvss": {"score": 3.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 3.3, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-732", "name": "Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource"}], "description": "The nohup utility in uutils coreutils creates its default output file, nohup.out, without specifying explicit restricted permissions. This causes the file to inherit umask-based permissions, typically resulting in a world-readable file (0644). In multi-user environments, this allows any user on the system to read the captured stdout/stderr output of a command, potentially exposing sensitive information. This behavior diverges from GNU coreutils, which creates nohup.out with owner-only (0600) permissions.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00114, "percentile": 0.01786}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-5hgf-628x-mcqf", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-30T17:50:53Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5hgf-628x-mcqf", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-5hgf-628x-mcqf"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-35367"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-22T17:16:40Z", "published_at": "2026-04-22T18:31:45Z", "references": ["https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-35367", "https://github.com/uutils/coreutils/issues/10021", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5hgf-628x-mcqf"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "low", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/uutils/coreutils", "summary": "uutils coreutils has an Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T17:50:53Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-5hgf-628x-mcqf", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "coreutils"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 0.8.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}
